content:
POPS＆アニメ (POPS & ANIME) is maimai’s category for licensed mainstream music, including J-pop hits, television show themes, and anime opening and ending tracks. This category is distinct from the game's original compositions and specifically features music from external media franchises and commercial artists such as Ado, Eve, and YOASOBI. In the current maimai でらっくす (maimai DX) version, it occupies one of the primary slots in the song selection menu and frequently receives new additions based on trending anime series and charts.

Historically, this category has served as a consistent entry point for casual players due to the familiarity of the licensed content. While other categories like niconico & VOCALOID or Touhou Project focus on internet-born subcultures, POPS & ANIME focuses on broad commercial media. In recent updates to the DX series, the category has also integrated various Virtual YouTuber (VTuber) songs and media project tracks that fall under general pop appeal, though some of these are occasionally moved or cross-listed depending on the specific collaboration. The category remains one of the most frequently updated sections of the game as licenses are added or expired over time.

niconico & VOCALOID is maimai’s category for songs sourced from the Niconico video platform and tracks featuring Vocaloid voice synthesis software, distinct from POPS & ANIME and Touhou Project. This category contains music produced by independent creators and virtual singers that gained popularity through internet communities. The current official song list in maimai でらっくす uses niconico & VOCALOID as a primary classification for these licensed works.

Historically, the category was initially labeled "niconico" in earlier versions of the game before being renamed to include VOCALOID. In the initial launch of maimai でらっくす, the name was reverted back to "niconico," but was later changed to "niconico & VOCALOID" starting with the maimai でらっくす PLUS update. This naming convention has remained consistent in the international and Japanese versions of the game to cover both general internet-based music and specific vocal synth content.

東方Project (Touhou Project) is maimai’s category for musical arrangements and remixes of the soundtracks from the Touhou Project bullet hell franchise. It consists exclusively of fan-made covers and doujin music based on the original compositions by ZUN, rather than tracks from the official games themselves. In the current maimai でらっくす (maimai DX) song list, this category is maintained as a standalone division, separating these specific community-driven arrangements from other licensed works like VOCALOID or general game music.

Historically, this category has remained one of the most stable classifications in the series due to the sheer volume of Touhou content available. While other categories have undergone mergers or renaming, the Touhou Project tab has consistently appeared as a primary selection since its introduction in early versions like maimai GreeN. In the transition to the DX series, it retained its independent status to accommodate the extensive library of tracks from famous circles such as IOSYS, COOL&CREATE, and Alstroemeria Records. The category continues to receive regular updates with both classic arrangements and new contest-winning entries.

ゲーム＆バラエティ (GAME & VARIETY) is maimai’s category for songs from other games and miscellaneous external/independent music, separate from maimai original songs and ONGEKI & CHUNITHM crossover songs. The current official song list still uses GAME & VARIETY as one of the main categories.

Historically, ゲーム＆バラエティ was introduced in maimai PiNK by merging the older GAME and VARIETY categories. In maimai でらっくす, it was temporarily renamed VARIETY, then changed back to GAME & VARIETY in maimai でらっくす PLUS.

maimai (maimai) is the category dedicated to original tracks composed specifically for the game series by SEGA's internal sound team and commissioned artists, serving as the core identity of the franchise's music library. These songs are distinct from licensed external content and include long-standing fan favorites and "boss tracks" that define the game's difficulty peaks. In the current maimai でらっくす (maimai DX) interface, this category is one of the primary tabs, housing hundreds of exclusive compositions that have debuted throughout the series' history.

Historically, this category has seen several organizational shifts as the series expanded its lineup of original music. In earlier versions, it simply contained all SEGA-original content, but with the launch of the maimai でらっくす series, it was refined to separate these tracks from other SEGA rhythm game crossovers found in the ONGEKI & CHUNITHM category. Despite these structural updates, the maimai category continues to be the definitive home for the game's mascot-themed songs and official tournament pieces, maintaining its status as a central pillar of the song selection screen across both Japanese and International versions.

オンゲキ＆CHUNITHM (ONGEKI & CHUNITHM) is maimai’s category for songs originating from SEGA’s other two major rhythm game titles, CHUNITHM and ONGEKI, as well as the multimedia project Irodori Midori. This section serves as a crossover hub, hosting internal SEGA compositions that were initially exclusive to these sister franchises before being ported to maimai. In the current maimai でらっくす (maimai DX) song list, this category is treated as a core division, grouping together various "Geki-Chu-Mai" collaboration tracks and characters under a single banner.

Historically, this category was created to organize the growing number of crossover tracks as SEGA's rhythm game ecosystem expanded. Before the launch of maimai でらっくす, many of these songs were scattered or grouped under the general "maimai" or "Sega Network" classifications. With the system overhaul in the DX series, the category was formalized to clarify the distinction between maimai-original music and ports from CHUNITHM and ONGEKI. It remains the primary location for Irodori Midori tracks and various competitive boss songs that have migrated across SEGA’s arcade platforms.

宴会場 (UTAGE) is maimai’s category for experimental and gimmick-heavy charts that feature non-standard gameplay mechanics not found in regular levels. These charts often include extreme note densities, drastic speed changes, and patterns designed specifically for two players to coordinate on a single cabinet. Because of their irregular nature, play results in the UTAGE category do not affect a player's official rating or total score.

Historically, the UTAGE category was a prominent feature in the "Original" timeline of maimai (such as maimai PiNK through FiNALE) but was removed during the initial transition to maimai でらっくす (maimai DX). It made a return in the maimai でらっくす BUDDiES update, introducing new "Buddy Score" mechanics where two players' achievement rates are summed together. Access to this category typically requires a player to have reached a specific Rating threshold, such as 10,000, and it remains a separate section from the standard difficulty tiers.
